blurb:
Rreal Tacos is one of those places the neighborhood keeps to itself but probably should not. The vibe is loud and fun, the service is attentive, and the food actually lives up to what people say about it. Great for a group dinner or a casual night out when you want good food and a couple of good drinks.

Start with the Esquite before anything else. For drinks, the Pineapple Guava pitcher and the Passion Fruit pitcher are both worth ordering, especially if you are sharing with the table. They are refreshing and well-balanced without being too sweet.

They are open until midnight every night except Sunday, so it is also a solid late-night option if you need one.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This little spot on Roswell Road is something genuinely different for East Cobb. It's a Brazilian açaí shop, and if you've never had açaí done right, this is the place to try it. The vibe is cozy and the staff is super friendly. Great for an afternoon snack, a post-workout treat, or bringing the kids after school.

The açaí is self-serve, which means you load it up with whatever toppings you want and as much as you like. They also serve crepes and coxinha, which is a popular Brazilian fried snack. It's worth branching out beyond the açaí bowl if you're feeling adventurous.

They're closed on Tuesdays but open until 10pm every other day, which makes it a nice option for a late-evening dessert run.
address: 2145 Roswell Rd Suite 110, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Sunnyside is the kind of neighborhood pizza spot you end up at over and over again without getting tired of it. It's tucked into Sandy Springs Circle and has a great casual vibe, good for a family dinner, a date night that doesn't need to be a big production, or just grabbing a pie on a weeknight when nobody wants to cook.

The Hot Honey Charred pizza gets talked about a lot for good reason, and the Hot Honey Lemon Pepper Chicken Wings are worth ordering before it even arrives. People also love the Caesar Salad, which sounds simple but apparently they do it really well. The wings are sweet with a little kick, and reviewers mention saving the sauce to dip their crust in.

They're closed on Mondays, so plan accordingly.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir Suite 149, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
When you need a real occasion restaurant and don't want to fight Buckhead traffic, Ray's on the River in Sandy Springs is the answer. It sits right on the Chattahoochee and the setting is genuinely beautiful. This is the kind of place you take out-of-town guests, celebrate a big anniversary, or treat someone when you want them to feel special.

The menu focuses on Southern seafood, and with over 11,000 reviews and a 4.6 rating, people keep coming back. It's on the pricier side, so go in knowing that, but most people feel it's worth it for the right occasion.

Sunday hours are split with a break between 4pm and 5pm, so plan your timing if you're going for Sunday dinner.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
Canton Cooks is right on Roswell Road and it's the kind of place you go when you want Chinese food that goes beyond the usual takeout routine. They do hot pot, BBQ duck, and lobster in black bean sauce, which is not something you find at every Chinese spot in the area. Good for a dinner out when you want to try something a little different without driving far.

The menu is eclectic and there's enough variety to keep things interesting whether you're a regular or a first-timer. It's a casual place that works well for a family dinner or a low-key night out with friends.

Open every day until 10pm, which makes it a reliable option when you decide late that you don't feel like cooking.
address: 5984 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Los Bravos has been a go-to for a lot of East Cobb families for years, and the food from the kitchen is why. It's a solid, affordable Mexican spot that works well for a weeknight dinner or bringing a group. Open every day and late on weekends, so it's easy to fit in.

The food is the draw here. Long-time regulars say the kitchen is consistently good and worth coming back for.

One honest note from reviewers: the margarita prices have gone up quite a bit lately and some folks feel they're higher than comparable spots nearby. Go for the food and just keep an eye on the drink tab if that matters to your budget.
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Royal Spice is a solid Indian restaurant in Sandy Springs that's worth knowing about if you haven't been. The staff is friendly and genuinely helpful if you have questions about the menu, which is a nice touch when you're trying something new or deciding between dishes. It's a good spot for a dinner out with the family or a casual date night.

The entrees and appetizers both get high marks from regulars. The atmosphere is relaxed with quiet music in the background, so it's easy to have a conversation without raising your voice.

They close between lunch and dinner most days, with the kitchen open 11am to 2:30pm and back up at 4:30pm. Worth keeping in mind if you're planning a meal.
address: 220 Hammond Dr #302, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you live anywhere near Roswell Road and need a pizza night fix, JJ's should be your first call. It's a no-frills spot in a strip mall, but the pizza is the real deal. Good for a casual family dinner or when you just want something reliable delivered to your door.

The stuffed pizza is what people are talking about, and for good reason. It's massive and one slice genuinely fills you up. If you haven't tried it yet, start there. The regular pies are solid too, and reviewers say they've never had a bad order.

Just a heads up that they're closed on Tuesdays, so plan accordingly.
address: 2211 Roswell Rd suite 116, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Heirloom Market BBQ is one of those places that regulars treat like a personal discovery they don't want too many people to know about. It's a tiny spot near the Akers Mill area with a single communal table, so don't go expecting a big dining room. But if you're serious about BBQ, it's worth the trip. The twist here is a Korean influence mixed into the BBQ, which sounds odd but works really well.

People rave about this place, and with a 4.7 rating across nearly 3,000 reviews, it's not hype. It's a great lunch run or early dinner spot, especially on a weekday when you want something a little different.

They're only open Tuesday through Saturday and close at 8pm, and they're done for the weekend on Sunday and Monday. Go early because they sometimes sell out.
address: 2243 Akers Mill Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
SATE is a little gem on Roswell Road that a lot of people drive past without knowing what they're missing. The owner is genuinely warm and takes pride in what they do here. If you've never had pho before, this is actually a great first stop. Multiple reviewers mention that they make it the traditional way, and you can taste the difference.

The pho is the thing to order, and it's priced really well for how good it is. It's the kind of place you go on a weeknight when you want something comforting and flavorful without spending much. Open every day from 10am to 9pm, which makes it easy to work into your week.

Parking is easy and the prices are very reasonable. Hard to beat for what you get.
address: 5980 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
